MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-07-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT SUMMERLIN LIFE AND HEALTH INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

APPEARANCES: Gary L. LaHendro, Esq. of Law Offices of Stephenson Acquisto & Colman 818/559-4477 for pltf & opposing party; George C. Harris, Esq. of Morrison & Foerster LLP 268-7328 for deft & moving party.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - granted. Equitable tolling does not apply in this case. The plaintiff did not pursue another remedy which would satisfy the first factor set forth in Tarkington v. Cal Unemployment Ins. Appeal Bd. (2009) 172 Cal. App. 4th 1494, 1503. Merely engaging in informal negotiations does not equitably toll the statute. See 65 Butterfield v. Chicago Title Ins. Co. (1999) 70 Cal. App. 4th 1047, 1063. Plaintiff's delay in filing this action after learning on August 28, 2008 that defendant paid the patient/member was not reasonable. Plaintiff had sufficient opportunity to commence a timely action after learning of the true facts. In addition, the Court states that equitable estoppel does not apply. Order & judgment signed in open court. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
